SEARCH
Package com.example.getoutthere.event

Class EventFilterFragment

java.lang.Object
androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
androidx.fragment.app.DialogFragment
androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatDialogFragment
com.google.android.material.bottomsheet.BottomSheetDialogFragment
com.example.getoutthere.event.EventFilterFragment
public class EventFilterFragment extends com.google.android.material.bottomsheet.BottomSheetDialogFragment
Represents the fragment used to configure and apply event search filters.
Version:
1.0

    Class
    Description
    static interface 
    EventFilterFragment.FilterListener
    Interface definition for a callback to be invoked when filters are applied.

  • Method Details

    • setFilterListener

      public void setFilterListener(EventFilterFragment.FilterListener listener)
      Sets the listener that will be notified when the user applies or clears the filters.
      Parameters:
      listener - The FilterListener implementation to receive filter updates.

    • onCreateView

      @Nullable public View onCreateView(@NonNull LayoutInflater inflater, @Nullable ViewGroup container, @Nullable Bundle savedInstanceState)
      Called to have the fragment instantiate its user interface view.
      Overrides:
      onCreateView in class androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
      Parameters:
      inflater - The LayoutInflater object that can be used to inflate any views in the fragment.
      container - If non-null, this is the parent view that the fragment's UI should be attached to.
      savedInstanceState - If non-null, this fragment is being re-constructed from a previous saved state.
      Returns:
      The View for the fragment's UI.

    • onViewCreated

      public void onViewCreated(@NonNull View view, @Nullable Bundle savedInstanceState)
      Called immediately after onCreateView has returned. Sets up the input fields, dropdown adapters, date pickers, and click listeners for the apply and clear buttons.
      Overrides:
      onViewCreated in class androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
      Parameters:
      view - The View returned by onCreateView.
      savedInstanceState - If non-null, this fragment is being re-constructed from a previous saved state.

    • showDatePicker

      private void showDatePicker(boolean isMin)
      Opens a DatePickerDialog and stores the selected date as milliseconds, displaying the formatted date string in the appropriate input field.
      Parameters:
      isMin - true if picking the minimum start date, false for the maximum start date.
